title: The Stockdale Paradox
author: From: Good to Great
content: You must maintain unwavering faith that you will prevail, while simultaneously confronting the most brutal facts of your current reality.

title: The Margin of Safety
author: From: The Art of Thinking Clearly
content: In life and strategy, always build in a buffer. If you operate at the absolute edge of your capacity, a single surprise will destroy your plan.

title: Redundancy for Resilience
author: From: Antifragile
content: Efficiency is the enemy of reliability. Redundancy—having a backup system—is the only way to survive the unknown.

title: Negative Visualization
author: From: A Guide to the Good Life
content: Imagine the loss of what you currently value. This prevents complacency and prepares you for the inevitable changes in life.

title: Second-Order Thinking
author: From: The Art of Thinking Clearly
content: Don't just ask 'and then what?'. Ask 'and then what after that?'. Poor decision-makers only see the immediate consequence.
